stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
The pilot used their EFB to make some calculations prior to their flight. These are the calculated V-speeds:

V1: 142 kt
VR: 150 kt
V2: 153 kt

If by error, the pilots enter a V1 value of 124 kts accidentally, what would the consequences on take-off performance be?

options.a: If one engine fails before 142 kt, the take-off distance may be significantly longer and the aeroplane may fail to become airborne before the end of the runway.
options.b: There is NO effect on the take-off distance following an engine failure at 130 kt, but the accelerate-stop distance will reduce following an engine failure at 130 kt.
options.c: There is NO effect on the all-engine take-off distance, but the accelerate-stop distance will increase if one engine fails before the V1 entered into the FMS.
options.d: The take-off run will decrease since the time to accelerate to 124 kt is shorter than to the calculated V1, but the take-off distance will increase because of the lower lift-off speed.
